Title: Discrete bHLH transcription factors play functionally overlapping roles in pigmentation patterning in flowers of Antirrhinum majus

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NADPH-dependent oxidoreductase; short-chain dehydrogenase/reductase-like DFR family
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: JI:522/JI:7 derived lines; N. benthamiana 'Northern Territory'
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in pigmented floral tissues; strongly reduced in inc I:del and roseadorsea mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In A. majus petals, transcript strongly associated with anthocyanin-pigmented tube/lobe tissues; in luciferase assays promoter activated in N. benthamiana leaves by Ros1 with Del or Inc I-containing complexes
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream to downstream committed late biosynthetic step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Floral pigmentation important for pollinator attraction and aesthetic appeal
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required late biosynthetic step directly supplying anthocyanin precursors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DFR expression is induced by Del and Inc I; its promoter architecture helps determine bicolour pigmentation patterning
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting; treated as a key regulated late biosynthetic target
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light affected pigmentation of weak inc I2/mutabilis phenotype in field vs glasshouse; transient agroinfiltration in N. benthamiana used for promoter tests
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native DFR promoter variants/deletions in luciferase reporters; effectors driven by double CaMV 35S
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR of endogenous transcripts; dual luciferase assays with DFR promoter deletions in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DFR is a direct major target of MBW regulation in A. majus petals. 2. Del and Inc I both require the promoter G-box 1 region for strong activation with Ros1. 3. AC-box/G-box arrangement determines differential responsiveness to Del- versus Inc I-containing complexes and contributes to bicolour patterning.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: DFR promoter cis-elements and its regulation by MBW components provide targets for tuning floral anthocyanin patterning in ornamentals
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to the mechanistic study, DFR itself was analyzed mainly as a biosynthetic target/promoter readout rather than the primary causal mutant gene.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Subgroup IIIf bHLH transcription factor, bHLH-2 clade (TT8/AN1-like)
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: JI:522 wild type; mutant alleles inc I1 and inc I2 in Antirrhinum lines
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: A. majus JI:7, JI:8, JI:522, JI:23 and related lines; N. benthamiana 'Northern Territory'
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in flower lobes and also in tubes of wild type; in del mutants detected only in lobes; transcript absent/reduced in inc I mutant alleles
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lobe-enriched functional activity; wild-type expression in both tubes and lobes, but del mutation abolishes tube expression; lower in roseadorsea
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis gene activation and floral pigment patterning
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as part of MBW complex; positively enhanced by WDR1 and positively regulated upstream by Del/MBW
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates late anthocyanin biosynthetic genes, especially DFR, F3H, ANS and 3GT
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MBW control via Del and Ros1; likely lobe-specific developmental inputs; possible contribution from Delila-like in lobes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Hierarchical feedforward regulation: Del/MBW promotes Inc I expression, enabling Inc I-containing MBW activity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in MBW complex with Ros1 and WDR1; WDR1 enhances Inc I activity
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Floral pigmentation patterning for pollinator attraction and ornamental value
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flower petal lobes predominantly, also contributes to tubes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for anthocyanin accumulation, especially in petal lobes; loss causes ivory flowers in del background and weak mutabilis/speckled phenotype in weak allele
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential lobe-specific bHLH regulator that explains residual lobe pigmentation of del mutants and is a major determinant of bicolour patterning
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Allelic analysis of classic mutants; mutation sequencing; biolistic complementation; qRT-PCR; promoter transactivation in N. benthamiana; anthocyanin induction assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Incolorata I encodes a bHLH-2 factor identified as the gene mutated in inc I and mutabilis. 2. It acts redundantly with Del to activate anthocyanin biosynthetic genes, especially in petal lobes. 3. Del positively regulates Inc I expression in tubes, creating hierarchical control that underlies bicolour flowers. 4. Inc I activity on DFR and anthocyanin accumulation is promoted by WDR1.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35Spro:Inc I in complementation/transient assays; acts on native DFR promoter variants
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light enhanced weak mutabilis/inc I2 lobe pigmentation in field-grown plants relative to glasshouse
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulator for engineering patterned anthocyanin pigmentation in ornamentals; likely most effective when combined with compatible MYB and WDR components and tissue-specific promoters
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Patterning depends on interaction context with Del, Ros1 and WDR1; weak allele expression is light-sensitive
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequence context from A. majus genome v3; qRT-PCR transcript profiling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: inc I1 carries a 4-bp duplication in exon 3 causing frameshift; inc I2 weak phenotype likely results from strongly reduced transcript accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Am02g33340; del23 genomic DNA GenBank MW027119
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Subgroup IIIf bHLH transcription factor, bHLH-1 clade (R/JAF13-like)
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: JI:7/JI:522 related wild-type lines; alleles del, delrec, del23
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: A. majus JI:8, JI:23 and related lines; N. benthamiana 'Northern Territory'
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Acts in both tube and lobe tissues, with stronger contribution in tubes; transcript not altered in rosdor
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional dominance in flower tubes; also active in lobes but partially redundant with Inc I
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis gene activation and hierarchical regulation of other MBW components
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of biosynthetic targets and positive upstream regulation of Inc I and WDR1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR; F3H; ANS; 3GT; Incolorata I; WDR1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates late anthocyanin biosynthetic genes and positively promotes Inc I and WDR1 expression, especially in tubes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Acts with Ros1; not under MBW control according to rosdor expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Hierarchical feedforward regulator promoting Inc I and WDR1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with Ros1 in MBW-like complex; WDR1 does not enhance and may inhibit Del-Ros1 output
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Floral coloration relevant to pollinator attraction and aesthetics
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major positive regulator of tube anthocyanin accumulation; del mutants show ivory tubes but retain lobe pigmentation due to Inc I
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Principal bHLH determinant of tube pigmentation and upstream hierarchical regulator shaping bicolour patterning
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Classical mutant genetics including revertants; complementation in petals; qRT-PCR; DFR promoter transactivation; transient anthocyanin induction in N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Del is a bHLH-1 regulator that activates anthocyanin biosynthesis in both tubes and lobes but is most important in tubes. 2. It acts redundantly with Inc I on biosynthetic genes such as DFR. 3. Del positively regulates Inc I and WDR1 expression, explaining hierarchical control and bicolour patterning. 4. Ros1-Del strongly induces anthocyanins, whereas WDR1 can reduce this output.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35Spro:Del in transient assays; acts on native DFR promoter variants
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering tube pigmentation or broad anthocyanin activation, but output depends on promoter context and WDR1 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss gives bicolour flowers; some alleles (del23) are weak due to mis-splicing; WDR1 may inhibit Del-mediated output in some contexts
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequence context from A. majus genome v3; qRT-PCR transcript profiling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: del23 is a weak allele with misspliced transcripts caused by intronic insertion near splice acceptor; delrec revertant sectors restore pigmentation.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: roseadorsea mutant and wild type; N. benthamiana 'Northern Territory'
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcripts present in both tube and lobe tissues; abundance unaffected by del and not significantly changed in inc I:del
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broad floral petal expression in tubes and lobes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis activation through MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation with bHLH partners on target promoters
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR and other anthocyanin biosynthetic genes via MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates DFR promoter alone to some extent in N. benthamiana and strongly with Del or Inc I
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Not under MBW control in this study
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MBW complex with Del or Inc I and WDR1-dependent/independent variants
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Floral pigmentation for pollinator attraction and ornamental quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive MYB regulator required for strong expression of late anthocyanin genes; rosdor mutants strongly reduce F3H, DFR, ANS and 3GT expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major MYB activator partnering with bHLHs to drive anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant transcript analysis; transient luciferase assays; anthocyanin induction in N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ros1 is a key MYB activator of anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. It activates DFR most strongly with Del or Inc I. 3. Its transcript abundance is not controlled by Del or Inc I, placing it outside the hierarchical loop described for Del→Inc I/WDR1.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be combined with specific bHLH partners to modulate intensity and pattern of anthocyanin accumulation in ornamental engineering
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Partner-specific output differs; with Del, WDR1 can reduce activity in heterologous assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states Ros1 has the strongest activity among the A. majus floral MYBs mentioned.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: A. majus wild type and mutants; N. benthamiana 'Northern Territory'
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in petals; transcripts reduced in tubes of del mutants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Petal expression with reduced tube expression in del mutant background
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Assembly/modulation of MBW complexes controlling anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive co-activation with Inc I-containing complex; inhibitory or nonenhancing effect with Del-containing complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances DFR promoter activation by Ros1-Inc I, but not by Ros1-Del; can repress net anthocyanin output with Ros1-Del
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Positively regulated in part by Del/MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of hierarchical network downstream of Del
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Core MBW complex component with Ros1 and Inc I/Del
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Floral pigmentation for pollinator attraction and ornamental value
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports Inc I-dependent anthocyanin activation but can dampen Del-dependent anthocyanin induction; contributes to bicolour patterning through differential complex behavior
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent MBW cofactor that is particularly important for full Inc I activity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA isolation; qRT-PCR; dual luciferase assays; transient anthocyanin induction assays in N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WDR1 is a WD40 MBW component in A. majus. 2. Its expression is reduced in del mutant tubes, indicating partial Del-dependent regulation. 3. WDR1 enhances Inc I-Ros1 activity on DFR and anthocyanin production, but does not enhance and may inhibit Del-Ros1 output.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Should be considered selectively when stacking anthocyanin regulators, as it benefits Inc I-containing complexes more than Del-containing ones
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May suppress or fail to enhance Del-mediated anthocyanin output depending on promoter and heterologous context
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as an AN11 homolog and likely scaffold/cofactor rather than DNA-binding TF.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Subgroup IIIf bHLH transcription factor, bHLH-1 clade
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: A. majus petals; N. benthamiana 'Northern Territory'
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low transcript abundance in both tubes and lobes, somewhat higher in lobes; detected in wild type and del mutants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low expression in floral tissues; not strongly MBW regulated
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Possible hierarchical regulation of anthocyanin patterning rather than strong direct activation of biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Putative transcriptional activation of regulatory genes; direct activation of anthocyanin biosynthesis appears weak/negligible
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Not established; proposed to promote lobe-specific Inc I expression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible hierarchical role upstream of Inc I in lobes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Can functionally complement anthocyanin production in bombarded petal cells, but weak in transient leaf assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely negligible direct role in anthocyanin biosynthesis but may contribute indirectly to lobe-specific patterning through regulation of Inc I
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent minor/putative regulator, not essential for direct pigment biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome identification; cDNA amplification; biolistic complementation; qRT-PCR; transient anthocyanin induction assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Del-like is a third subgroup IIIf bHLH in A. majus and clusters with the bHLH-1 clade. 2. It can complement anthocyanin production in individual petal cells when overexpressed, but weakly affects anthocyanin accumulation in N. benthamiana leaves. 3. The authors propose it may regulate Inc I expression in lobes rather than directly drive anthocyanin biosynthetic genes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for subtle or tissue-specific pattern engineering, but direct efficacy on anthocyanin production appears weak
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Weak direct activity may limit utility; role remains partly inferential
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR transcript profiling; genome sequence context from A. majus genome v3
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulator because it was experimentally tested in results, although its direct metabolic role is minor and partly hypothetical.
